what_it_does: Defines the icon language for the ConceptV design system: CSS custom properties for icon sizes (xs-xl), stroke weights, line caps/joins, and ink/accent colours, plus an `.icon` base class and modifiers (size, bold, muted, accent), a two-tone `.ic-accent` rule, and an `.icon-box` chip wrapper that places a glyph in a tinted square or circle.
observations: Icon size scale: --icon-xs 14px through --icon-xl 32px, with md (20px) as the default inline size
Stroke weights given as unitless numbers (1.6 default, 2.2 bold) intended for a 24-unit SVG grid
Authoring contract stated in the header comment: inline <svg class="icon"> on a 24 grid, fill=none, stroke=currentColor
Two-tone treatment: neutral stroke plus a gold-filled accent sub-path via class="ic-accent"
--icon-box-bg uses color-mix(in oklch, currentColor 11%, transparent) for a tint derived from inherited ink
.icon-box inherits zone ink via --zone-ink with --accent-bronze fallback; child .icon set to color:inherit
Tokens reference upstream vars (--ink-2, --ink-4, --accent-gold) defined elsewhere
standouts: color-mix(oklch) used to derive the box tint from currentColor rather than a fixed value
Header comment frames gold as 'the decision colour' giving the accent a semantic meaning beyond aesthetics
conventions: Follows token-then-class pattern: :root custom properties, then classes that var() them
Carries @kind other annotations on unitless/non-length token values
References roles (--ink-2, --accent-gold) rather than raw values, per the design system's single-home rule
notes: The single home for inline-icon sizing, stroke weight, two-tone gold accent, and the boxed-icon chip; SVGs must be authored on a 24 grid with currentColor stroke.
questions: Where are the referenced upstream tokens (--ink-2, --ink-4, --accent-gold, --accent-bronze, --zone-ink, --r-sm) defined?
Is --icon-box (used as the box dimension fallback 32px) defined anywhere or always defaulted?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header states an icon language with consistent grid/stroke/two-tone, and the file delivers exactly those tokens and classes.
intention_signals: Trying to make every glyph look uniform (one grid, one stroke weight, one cap style) while letting size and colour flow from context, and to give the accent fill a single semantic colour tied to decisions.
novelty: The oklch color-mix tint derived from currentColor and the explicit 'gold = decision colour' framing for the accent.
contribution: Contributes the inline-icon sub-scale and stroke/cap conventions plus the two-tone accent and boxed-icon chip primitives — the glyph-within-chrome sizing layer distinct from whole-element sizing.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS defining icon size/stroke/cap tokens, an .icon base class with size/bold/muted/accent modifiers, a two-tone gold accent rule, and an .icon-box tinted-chip wrapper.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce a consistent hand-drawn icon language (24-grid, currentColor stroke, gold accent) sized and coloured from context via single-source tokens.
